fact: Dresden had minimal anti-aircraft defenses at the time of the bombing
The Allies sent two waves of bombers, with the second wave timed to target emergency responders
source: Among the Dead Cities, A.C. Grayling (2006); also documented in RAF Bomber Command planning records

fact: Hundreds of thousands of refugees had swelled Dresden's population, fleeing the advancing Eastern Front
source: Dresden: Tuesday, February 13, 1945, Frederick Taylor (2004). Estimates of refugees range widely; Taylor discusses the influx in detail.

fact: Incendiary bombs created a firestorm with hurricane-force winds pulling inward toward the center of the blaze
source: The Fire: The Bombing of Germany, 1940–1945, Jörg Friedrich (2006). Firestorm mechanics also described in official RAF post-raid analyses.

fact: Approximately 25,000 people were killed in the Dresden bombing
source: Dresden Commission of Historians (Historikerkommission Dresden), final report (2010), commissioned by the city of Dresden, concluded up to 25,000 deaths
